templates.use_when: First reply, customer calm or mildly frustrated
templates.subject: Re: your experience with [Business]
templates.body:
Hi [Name],

Thank you for reaching out. I'm sorry [specific issue] happened: that's not the experience we aim for.

I've looked into this: [1-2 sentences on facts].

Here's what I'm doing: [specific fix] by [date].

If anything changes or this doesn't fix it, reply to this email and it comes straight to me.

[Your name]
[Business]

templates.use_when: Repeat contact, missed deadline, or rising frustration
templates.subject: Update on your complaint (ref #)
templates.body:
Hi [Name],

I owe you an update. You contacted us on [date] about [issue]. You were right to expect [X] by [date]: we missed that.

What happened: [brief honest cause, no jargon].

What we're doing now: [action] by [new date]. I've assigned this to [name/role].

I'll follow up again on [date] even if it's resolved, so you're not wondering.

[Your name]

templates.use_when: Serious failure, safety, legal tone, or owner escalation
templates.subject: Personal follow-up from [Owner/Manager] ([Business])
templates.body:
Hi [Name],

[Owner name] here. I've read your account of [issue] and I'm sorry. We failed you on [specific point].

I'm not asking you to accept a generic response. Here's what I've personally approved: [remedy].

Please contact me directly at [phone/email] if this isn't resolved to your satisfaction by [date].

We track every complaint like this so it doesn't repeat.

[Name], [Title]
[Business], this is [Name]. How can I help you today?

templates.use_when: Need time to check system or person
templates.body: I'm still here: checking [system/person]. Thanks for your patience.
Hi [Name]: [Your name] from [Business]. Checking in: did [fix] work? Reply if anything's still off. Thank you again for your patience.
Thank you for telling us. I'm going to take this seriously. Tell me what happened, from the start. I'm listening.
So if I understand: [repeat issue]. That would frustrate me too. I'm sorry you had that experience.
Let me check [order / appointment / account] so we're working from facts. Give me [X minutes].
Here's what I can do: [specific action] by [date/time]. Does that work for you?
I'm logging this so we follow up. Expect [email/call] from [name] by [time]. Is [contact] still best?

templates.use_when: Only after customer satisfied, never during heat
templates.body: We're glad we could make this right. If you'd be willing to share that we fixed it, it helps other customers know we stand behind our work. No pressure either way.
